message:
test(fcp-e2e): wrap Airtable/GoogleDocs/GoogleSheets/S3 adapter connectors in Mutex

These four e2e adapters kept a bare Inner connector inside the
FcpConnector impl:

    struct FooConnectorAdapter {
        connector: FooConnector,
        id: ConnectorId,
    }

That works under the old e2e harness where requests were driven
serially. The compliance harness is evolving to exercise adapters
from multiple tasks (concurrent invoke + handshake + health in the
same suite run), and `&self` handle_*() paths that mutate internal
state race under the current borrow shape — specifically handle_configure
and handle_handshake take `&mut self` on the inner connector, which
cannot coexist with handle_health's `&self` across overlapping
awaits in the adapter.

Wrap the inner connector in `fcp_async_core::sync::Mutex` so every
handle_*() call goes through a guarded `.lock().await` before
delegating. Changes per adapter:

- struct field `connector: FooConnector`
  → `connector: Mutex<FooConnector>`
- ctor wraps the constructor in Mutex::new(...)
- configure / handshake / handle_health / shutdown / invoke all
  gain `.connector.lock().await` before calling the inner method.
  invoke acquires the lock once per call (not per sub-step) so the
  connector's own internal state-machine ordering is preserved.
- handle_health is now routed through a short-lived lock scope so
  it doesn't block with invoke in flight.

Matches the shape that DocuSign / LlmRouter adapters are already
using (they never held the bare connector; the mutex there is at
the verifier layer). Does not change connector semantics —
handle_*() are still called with the same arguments, in the same
order, with the same error surface.

Note: this commit only changes adapter wrapping. The adjacent
capability-token refactor (separate commit) handles the
req.capability_token move → borrow for DocuSign / LlmRouter; the
four adapters touched here still move capability_token by owned
value and will be migrated to `&req.capability_token` in a
follow-up once the full set of call sites is audited.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
